summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host info and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json line 30 declares a lifecycle hook that loads child_process, os, and https, collects host identifiers (hostname, cwd, process.env) and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds the harm: it requires fs/os/https/child_process at the top, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync checks (lines 93, 114), runs execSync('whoami') and os.hostname() to gather host identity (lines 233-246), shells out to curl (lines 298, 323, 408), and POSTs the collected data via https.request to the same hardcoded endpoint https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-161, 323). The destination is a GitHub Pages personal site unrelated to any legitimate web3 secrets-detection vendor; the package's stated purpose (a 'secrets detector') is used as a cover story for actively exfiltrating the installer's host info, environment variables, and filesystem signals. This combines the hardcoded-C2, filesystem-network-exfiltration, and system-info-exfiltration fingerprints in both lifecycle (install/load) code and the package's main module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
